violations.text: The following utensils were observed improperly stored/used between uses: 1) Cups are being used in containers of food. Utensils used to dispense foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container 2) A scoop was observed stored in stagnant water between uses 3) Several knives were observed stored in unclean surface between equipment and wall Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2024

Mixing bowls were in poor repair (observed heavily pitted). PIC instructed to take equipment out of service. Per interview with PIC, new equipment has been ordered to replace existing equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicers have an accumulation of food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2024
The floors throughout the facility were observed in poor repair (several area missing adequate grout). New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of the can opener, slicers, and dicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC to inform staff of proper sanitizing procedures. Equipment to not be used until adequately sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

Rice, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 91 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Rice was placed back in microwave to reheat to 165. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

Sauce was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit on ice. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Ice rail was refilled with ice and water. PIC to instruct all staff on how to set up ice baths for each station. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

The door to the outside, located in the ware washing area, is not tight-fitting due to missing concrete. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/25/2023

The floors in the kitchen are constructed of worn down grout which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. Per PIC, establishment is planning on replacing the entire floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2023
